Term.definition: Composite types formed by combining other types, commonly using 'sum' and 'product' types.
A trait that can only be extended in the same file, enabling exhaustive pattern matching.
Enum or class values that accept type or value parameters for greater flexibility.
A special class in Scala for immutable data, automatically providing useful methods like equals and copy.
A singleton object in Scala with pattern matching support, similar to a case class but without parameters.
An object whose state cannot be changed after it is created.
A special method used to initialize a newly created object in a class.
Describes a method in a subclass that replaces a method with the same signature in its superclass.
The data stored within an object that determines its current condition or behavior.
A type that describes functions by specifying their parameter and return types.
When an inner function accesses variables from its enclosing scope, it is said to capture them.
The set of rules that defines the structure of statements in a programming language.
An abstract data type representing computations as a series of chained operations.
Syntax within a programming language designed to make things easier to read or express.
A Haskell syntax for sequencing monadic operations in a readable way.
An anonymous function that can be defined and used inline.
Relating to or using monads, often describing computations or operations.
A debugging tool that pauses program execution at a specific line for inspection.
A tool that helps inspect and control program execution to find and fix bugs.
A Scala construct to restrict which classes can extend a trait, enabling exhaustive pattern matching.
A special Scala class with built-in support for pattern matching and immutability.
A Scala type representing a value that may be present (Some) or absent (None).
Describes data that can be changed after its creation, as opposed to immutable data.
A higher-order function that applies a function and flattens the result, often used with collections.
Indicates evaluation is deferred until the value is actually needed, improving efficiency in some cases.
A Scala collection abstraction that enables lazy transformations without creating intermediate collections.
Composite data types formed by combining other types, often using sum and product types.
A tree representation of the syntactic structure of source code or expressions.
A function that takes other functions as parameters or returns a function as a result.
The ability of a variable or data structure to be changed after its creation.
The property of an object whose state cannot be modified after it is created.
A data structure representing a single function call's execution context on the call stack.
An error that occurs when the call stack exceeds its memory limit, often due to deep recursion.
A special form of metadata added to code elements, used by the compiler or tools for extra processing.
An object with the same name as a class, allowing shared access to private members.
The main constructor of a class, defined in the class signature in Scala.
A Scala type representing an optional String value; can be Some(value) or None.
A mechanism for checking a value against a pattern and deconstructing data structures.
A design pattern ensuring a class has only one instance in the application.
Java Virtual Machine, the runtime environment for executing Java and Scala bytecode.
A special data type that enables for a variable to be a set of predefined constants.
A property ensuring correct behavior when code is executed by multiple threads simultaneously.
Describes execution where multiple computations happen during overlapping time periods.
A method that collapses nested collections into a single collection by removing one level of nesting.
A special Scala class for immutable data with automatic equals, hashCode, and toString methods.
A Scala type that defines a set of named, constant values.
An unordered collection of unique elements in Scala.
A type that allows a value to be one of several specified types.
The empty list in Scala, representing the end of a linked list.
An operator that appends an element to the end of a collection in Scala.
Scala syntax that simplifies working with monads, allowing sequential operations in a readable way.
Language features that make code easier to read or write without adding new functionality.
A Scala library providing functional programming abstractions like monads and type classes.
A Scala library offering functional programming tools, including type classes and data structures.
A control flow mechanism where a function returns from an outer scope, not just the current function.
A collection where elements are computed only as needed, not all at once.
A collection that stores unique elements with fast lookup using a hash table.
A collection of key-value pairs with fast access, implemented using a hash table.
A function is pure if it has no side effects and depends only on its arguments.
Changes or interactions with external state or data outside a function's arguments.
Redefine a method in a subclass, replacing the implementation from the superclass.
A class that extends another class, inheriting its properties and methods.
A keyword used in a subclass to refer to the parent class's methods or constructors.
A new name for an existing type, used to improve code readability or abstraction.
Methods added to existing types without modifying their original definition, introduced in Scala 3.
A feature that checks a value against patterns and executes code based on the match.
A special data type that enables a variable to be a set of predefined constants.
A method that filters and transforms elements of a collection using pattern matching.
A container type that may hold a value (Some) or no value (None).
Represents an Option containing a value.
Represents an Option with no value.
Operations that directly manipulate individual bits of binary numbers.
The component of a color that represents its transparency level.
An observable interaction with the outside world, such as modifying a variable or performing I/O.
An object or variable whose state or value can be changed after creation.
A variable accessible from any part of the program, not limited to a specific scope.
An Option type representing a value that exists, used for optional values in Scala.
An Option type representing the absence of a value in Scala.
A method that applies a function to the value inside an Option or collection.
A method that returns the first element matching a condition in a collection.
A method that returns the first result of a partial function applied to a collection.
A special class in Scala for immutable data, supporting pattern matching.
A singleton instance in Scala, often used for utility methods or constants.
An ordered collection type in Scala, supporting indexed access.
A special method used to initialize a newly created object of a class.
A specific realization of any object or class in programming.
A variable used to pass information into a function or method.
An abstraction that allows chaining operations while handling context, such as errors or optionality, in functional programming.
A function defined only for certain input values, not necessarily all possible inputs of its type.
Entities that can be assigned to variables, passed as arguments, and returned from functions.
A mechanism for checking a value against a pattern and deconstructing data structures.
A popular Scala library providing functional programming abstractions like monads, functors, and type classes.
A trait in Scala representing a collection of elements that can be iterated over.
A special data type in Scala for defining a set of named constants.
A Scala class optimized for immutable data and pattern matching.
A Scala construct to define abstract interfaces that can be mixed into classes.
A singleton instance in Scala, used for utility methods or as a holder for values.
A mechanism for checking a value against patterns, often used for deconstructing data structures.
An object with the same name as a class, providing factory methods and shared functionality.
A design pattern or method used to create objects, often abstracting instantiation details.
An object with an unapply method used for pattern matching and data extraction in Scala.
A mechanism for checking a value against patterns, often using case statements.
A reusable collection of fields and methods that can be mixed into classes in Scala.
A modifier for methods or classes that are declared but not implemented, to be defined by subclasses.
Synchronization primitives that prevent multiple threads from accessing shared data simultaneously.
Concurrency constructs that combine mutual exclusion and the ability to wait for a condition.
A data structure that groups multiple elements, such as lists, sets, or maps.
A special data type that enables for a variable to be a set of predefined constants.
A special type of class in Scala optimized for immutable data and pattern matching.
A program state where execution is aborted due to a critical error.
A function not defined for all possible input values of its domain.
A function defined for every possible input value of its domain.
An abstraction that allows sequencing of computations, handling values and context together.
A special method or function used to create and initialize an object or instance.
A Scala construct similar to an interface, allowing code reuse and multiple inheritance.
A mechanism for checking a value against patterns, often used for branching logic.
A special data type that enables a variable to be a set of predefined constants.
A special type that defines a set of named values, called cases, in Scala.
A class in Scala optimized for immutable data and pattern matching.
An unordered collection of unique elements in Scala.
A Scala keyword used to bring members from a package or object into scope.
A function defined only for a subset of possible input values.
Extracting values from data structures, like tuples or case classes, into separate variables.
Special Scala classes optimized for pattern matching and immutability.
A pattern for creating a distinct type from an existing one without runtime overhead.
A special data type that enables for a variable to be a set of predefined constants.
An unordered collection of unique elements in Scala.
A function defined without a name, often used as a short, inline function.
A special Scala class for immutable data with built-in equals, hashCode, and pattern matching.
A Scala type representing a value that may be present (Some) or absent (None).
A technique where method calls are linked together in a single statement.
A special data type that enables a variable to be a set of predefined constants.
A type in Scala that defines fields and methods, which can be mixed into classes.
A special type of class in Scala optimized for pattern matching and immutability.
An ordered collection in Scala that allows indexed access to elements.
A method that applies a partial function to a collection, filtering and transforming elements.
A method that checks if a partial function is defined for a given input.
An immutable sequence collection in Scala, similar to a list.
An immutable, singly linked list collection in Scala.
A function that returns true or false for a given input, often used for filtering.
A data structure that holds multiple elements, like lists, sets, or arrays.
A computation that is delayed until its result is actually needed.
A computation that is performed immediately, rather than being delayed.
A method that returns the first element of a collection.
A function that returns true or false, often used to test conditions on data.
A property where an expression can be replaced with its value without changing program behavior.
A pattern in code that may indicate a deeper problem or poor design.
Observable effects of computation other than returning a value, like modifying variables or printing output.
A style or pattern of code that is considered natural and standard in a programming language.
A substring that appears at the beginning of another string.
A mechanism for checking a value against a pattern and deconstructing data structures.
A special method for creating and initializing a new instance of a class.
Classes that inherit properties and methods from another class, called the superclass.
A function defined for every possible input value of its domain.
A function not defined for all possible input values of its domain.
A collection of pre-written classes and functions included with a programming language.
A property where a computation can yield multiple possible results, not just one.
A higher-order function that applies a function and flattens the resulting collections into one.
A variable used to collect or accumulate a result across recursive or iterative calls.
A recursion where the recursive call is the last operation in the function.
A visibility modifier restricting access to members within the same class or object.
A file or grouping of code, often representing a namespace or unit of organization.
The rules that determine where variables or functions are accessible in code.
A function that takes other functions as parameters or returns a function.
A method that selects elements from a collection based on a predicate function.
A symbol (like _) used to indicate missing arguments in partially applied functions.
An ancient algorithm for finding all prime numbers up to a given limit.
An object with the same name as a class, defined in the same file, providing related methods.
A container type that may hold a value or be empty, representing optional values in Scala.
A mechanism for checking a value against patterns and executing code based on the match.
An additional boolean condition in a pattern match case to further filter matches.
The underscore (_) used to ignore a value or parameter in Scala expressions.
